description_long: This is a medium-sized, round yellow onion with a slightly flattened top and bottom. The surface is covered in a thin, papery, tan to light brown outer skin, which is partially peeling and reveals the smoother, glossier flesh beneath in some areas. The top of the onion shows remnants of the dried stem, while the base is slightly rough, indicating where the root tuft was once attached. The bulb is mostly continuous, with subtle vertical ridges hinting at the layered structure inside. The coloration varies from pale tan to light brown, with some areas appearing shinier due to the exposed inner layers. No green shoots or sprouting stems are visible.
description: A round, tan-yellow onion with partially peeling papery skin and a dried stem remnant on top. The surface shows both matte and glossy patches, revealing the layered structure beneath.
description_view_0: This view highlights the top of the onion with a prominent dried stem remnant and a mostly intact papery skin.
description_view_1: This side shows a vertical split in the papery skin, exposing more of the smooth, inner flesh and subtle ridges.
description_view_2: Here, the onion's surface is glossier, with a large patch of exposed inner layer and less papery skin coverage.
description_view_3: This angle displays the base of the onion, where the root tuft was attached, and a more uniform, matte skin coverage.
attribute_bulb_segmentation: The bulb appears as a single continuous structure with subtle vertical ridges; no distinct lobes or segments are clearly visible.
attribute_papery_outer_skin: The papery outer skin is partially intact, with some areas peeling away to reveal the glossier inner flesh. The skin is tan to light brown and has a dry, flaky texture.
attribute_root_tuft: A small, rough area at the base indicates where the root tuft was attached, but no visible roots remain.
attribute_green_shoot_presence: No green shoots or sprouting stems are visible.
attribute_location_relative_to_urban_ele: No urban elements or context are visible; the onion appears isolated on a neutral background with no indication of indoor or outdoor placement.
